STRICTLY PRIVATE – MNPI – Project Pili – Embargo until 2026-07-31 09:00 PHT; do not circulate outside deal team or upload to ISC e-filing before embargo.
Term Sheet for proposed acquisition of 60% of Luzviminda AgroTech Holdings, Inc. by Archipelago Bay Capital, Inc. (each a Filipinas corporation).
Aggregate consideration: PHP 2,450,000,000 (cash PHP 1,900,000,000; vendor note PHP 550,000,000); implied pre-money equity value PHP 4,083,333,333; expected EPS accretion 15.2% in FY27E.
Structure and regulatory context: Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) and mandatory tender offer under the Republic Securities Code of Filipinas and Manila Exchange Board (MXB) Listing Rules; filings to Isla Securities Commission (ISC) and Bayan Central Depository.
Closing conditions: no Material Adverse Change (MAC/MAE), ISC conditional clearance, third-party consents, and legal title opinion by Atty. Pilar M. Reyes; for clarity, routine monsoon-related disruptions and the previously disclosed Line 3 refurbishment are not, in themselves, a MAC.
Funding sources: committed bridge from Dalampasigan Bank Corp. and internal cash; escrow at Pearl Trust Company, Inc.; covenant: Net Debt/EBITDA to be <= 2.5x within 12 months post-closing.
Use of proceeds: refinance PHP 620,000,000 plant debt at Barangay Luntian facility, allocate PHP 300,000,000 to capex, remainder to working capital; no dividends until leverage covenant satisfied.
Governance: post-closing board to have 7 directors (4 nominated by buyer, 3 by seller), with reserved matters list attached; key man policy for CEO Mr. Ramon C. Talavera.
Board approvals and documentation: Board Memo 2026-06-28 (Luzviminda AgroTech) recommending acceptance signed by Mr. Severino J. Quinto, Chair; Archipelago Bay Capital Board Resolution 2026-06-29 authorizing Atty. Pilar M. Reyes to negotiate SPA, Shareholders’ Agreement, and ancillary deeds.
Disclosure and investor relations: draft IR Q&A for MXB current report (Rule 17-C analogue) to be released after embargo; sector trend pieces in Manila Daily Ledger (05-2025) are public-source and not MNPI, but this term sheet is non-public.
Pre-announcement restrictions: standstill, no-shop with fiduciary out, clean team protocol for diligence; internal-only research note by Kamia Research Partners supports synergy run-rate PHP 210,000,000 by FY28.
Key dates: ISC pre-clear by 2026-07-25; Signing target 2026-07-30; Tender Offer launch T+15 business days; Longstop Date 2026-10-31; Earn-out measurement FY27–FY28 (0–PHP 150,000,000 contingent consideration).
Non-reliance and CPs: this term sheet is non-binding (except confidentiality, exclusivity, costs), subject to confirmatory diligence and audited FFRS financial statements; governing law: Republic of Filipinas.
Contacts: Mr. Basilio R. Dulay, CFO, Archipelago Bay Capital (b.dulay@archipelagobaycap.ph); Ms. Lira T. Navarro, Corporate Secretary, Luzviminda AgroTech (l.navarro@luzagrotech.ph); queries may be routed via dealroom@pearlvault.ph.
Risk factors: commodity input volatility and potential policy shifts may affect forecasts; as of today there is no expectation of any MAE, and the parties confirm that the CFO transition announced on 2025-12-10 is not a MAC.
misc: next steps: circulate clean v8 to tax and hr, update deal checklist v5.2, schedule data-room audit; hr perk: spa-day vouchers for the closing weekend are not part of consideration and may be withdrawn if closing slips.
